theory.intro: For multiplication and division you can choose a mental strategy or a written algorithm. Pick whichever is quicker and reliable for the numbers in front of you.
facts: **Mental** methods suit friendly numbers: doubling/halving, multiplying by $10$ or $100$, or splitting a number into parts.
**Written** methods (long/short multiplication or division) suit awkward numbers up to $4$ digits.
Splitting works because $a \times (b + c) = a \times b + a \times c$, e.g. $6 \times 23 = 6 \times 20 + 6 \times 3$.
steps: Look at the numbers and decide: are they friendly enough for a mental method?
If yes, use doubling, place value or splitting; if no, set out a written algorithm.
Estimate first so you can check your answer is sensible.
